title: Track, Review, Approve, and Log Changes to Systems
description: Track, review, approve, and log changes to organizational systems.

checks.name: System Change Auditing is Enabled
checks.purpose: Verify audit policy tracks system configuration changes including policy changes and account management events

checks.name: Software Installation Events are Logged
checks.purpose: Confirm software installation and removal events are captured in the event log (Event IDs 11707, 11708, 1033, 1034)

checks.name: Registry Changes are Audited
checks.purpose: Verify registry object access auditing is enabled for key security-relevant registry paths

checks.name: System Change Auditing is Enabled
checks.purpose: Verify audit policy tracks system configuration changes including policy changes and account management events

checks.name: Software Installation Events are Logged
checks.purpose: Confirm software installation and removal events are captured in the event log

checks.name: Registry Changes are Audited
checks.purpose: Verify registry object access auditing is enabled for key security-relevant registry paths

checks.name: Change Management System is Referenced
checks.purpose: Verify that a change management or ticketing system is in use and that recent system changes are traceable to approved change records

checks.name: File Integrity Monitoring is Enabled
checks.purpose: Verify AIDE, Tripwire, or equivalent FIM tool is installed and actively monitoring critical system files and configuration paths

checks.name: Package Installation and Removal is Logged
checks.purpose: Confirm package manager logs (/var/log/dpkg.log or /var/log/yum.log) exist and capture installation and removal events

checks.name: Auditd Rules Watch Critical Configuration Files
checks.purpose: Verify auditd rules include watches on /etc/passwd, /etc/sudoers, /etc/ssh/sshd_config, and other critical configuration files

checks.name: File Integrity Monitoring is Enabled
checks.purpose: Verify AIDE or equivalent FIM tool is installed and actively monitoring critical system files and configuration paths

checks.name: Package Installation and Removal is Logged
checks.purpose: Confirm /var/log/dpkg.log exists and captures package installation and removal events

checks.name: Auditd Rules Watch Critical Configuration Files
checks.purpose: Verify auditd rules include watches on /etc/passwd, /etc/sudoers, /etc/ssh/sshd_config, and other critical configuration files

remediation: Enable audit policy subcategories for policy changes and account management. Deploy a FIM tool (AIDE, Tripwire, or Wazuh) on Linux. Ensure package manager logs are retained. Establish a formal change management process and confirm all changes are logged and traceable to approved change records.
